terms.definition: A typed superset of JavaScript that adds compile-time static typing and compiles to plain JavaScript.
A TypeScript construct describing the shape of an object; can be extended and merged.
A named type defined with the type keyword, able to express unions, intersections, tuples and primitives.
A type that allows only specific literal values, e.g. 'user' | 'assistant'.
A safe top type that accepts any value but must be narrowed before use, unlike any.
Type compatibility based on a value's shape rather than its declared name.
The removal of all type information during compilation, so types have no runtime presence.
